channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-07-02
item.description:
Our open-source research community has seen exciting progress across multiple projects. The `scienceverse/metacheck` team has made significant strides in refining its Crossref API interaction, implementing a prevention mechanism for duplicate API requests and adding error logging for better debugging. Meanwhile, the `Davidvandijcke/coarse` project has achieved notable milestones, including localizing the confirmation email and review page, as well as enabling RLS on the schema_migrations bookkeeping table. Interestingly, the `JamieCummins/regcheck` project has removed its sign-up feature, potentially streamlining its workflow. These updates demonstrate the community's commitment to enhancing reliability, efficiency, and user experience across our research tools. As our projects continue to evolve, we look forward to exploring potential connections and collaborations between them, driving innovation and progress in the scientific community.

39 commits across 4 active repositories this week.

Most active:

regcheck19 commits
docpluck11 commits
escicheck5 commits
metacheck4 commits

item.title: docpluck: 6 commits today
item.description:
giladfeldman/docpluck

Work was done on the 'main' branch, where documentation changes were made, including archiving source findings and specifications, and removing resolved reconciliation handoffs, as stated in the commit "docs: archive source FINDINGS + spec, remove resolved reconciliation handoffs". The 'main' branch also saw the addition of a new CHANGELOG for version 2.4.101, along with reconciliation findings, as per the commit "docs(release): v2.4.101 CHANGELOG + reconciliation FINDINGS". Fixes were applied to tables, including stripping long caption-tail prose headers and version bump to 2.4.101, as mentioned in the commit "fix(tables): strip LONG caption-tail prose header + version bump v2.4.101", and also fixing efendic T3 running-header and T2-5 caption-tail-prose strip, as stated in "fix(tables): efendic T3 running-header + T2-5 caption-tail-prose strip". Additionally, work-in-progress changes were made to reconcile concurrent table-capture sessions, as noted in the commit "wip(tables): reconcile concurrent table-capture sessions onto greedy+rescue". Other fixes included addressing duplicate-fragment pairing issues in version 2.4.100, as mentioned in "fix(tables): v2.4.100 — bmc_med_3 duplicate-fragment pairing (narrow duplicate-starvation rescue)". The 'main' branch also saw a feature addition of region-driven Camelot capture with content-plausibility guards, as stated in the commit "feat(tables): region-driven Camelot capture + content-plausibility guards (DP-1/DP-2)".

6 commit(s) today:

  • 1a7f8be [main] docs: archive source FINDINGS + spec, remove resolved reconciliation handoffs — Gilad Feldman
  • 0f5dba9 [main] docs(release): v2.4.101 CHANGELOG + reconciliation FINDINGS — Gilad Feldman
  • 53fde8d [main] fix(tables): strip LONG caption-tail prose header + version bump v2.4.101 — Gilad Feldman
  • 819902e [main] fix(tables): efendic T3 running-header + T2-5 caption-tail-prose strip — Gilad Feldman
  • c7246f4 [main] wip(tables): reconcile concurrent table-capture sessions onto greedy+rescue — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: escicheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
giladfeldman/escicheck

The `main` branch was updated with two commits. The first commit synced changes from `escicheckapp@edb3c7b`, which corresponds to `effectcheck v0.6.12`, as stated in the commit message. The second commit, also on the `main` branch, synced changes from `escicheckapp@928a5bc`, relating to `effectcheck v0.6.11`. These commits brought the `main` branch in line with specific versions of `effectcheck`. The commits specifically reference external versions `v0.6.12` and `v0.6.11` of `effectcheck`.

1 commit(s) today:

  • e09dbc9 [main] sync from escicheckapp@edb3c7b (effectcheck v0.6.12) (#25) — Gilad Feldman
  • bedc262 [main] sync from escicheckapp@928a5bc (effectcheck v0.6.11) (#24) — Gilad Feldman
  • cc5cfd0 [main] sync from escicheckapp@1d4c2dd (effectcheck v0.6.9) (#23) — Gilad Feldman
  • 75ba643 [main] sync from escicheckapp@26f8430 (effectcheck v0.6.8) (#22) — Gilad Feldman
  • a154bdb [main] sync from escicheckapp@57a791d (effectcheck v0.6.7) (#21) — Gilad Feldman
  • 244b2d8 [main] sync from escicheckapp@f37b5a1 (effectcheck v0.6.6) (#20) — Gilad Feldman
  • a588210 [main] sync from escicheckapp@bd6f007 (effectcheck v0.6.5) (#19) — Gilad Feldman
  • 1d48c4e [main] sync from escicheckapp@dec37b1 (effectcheck v0.6.4) (#18) — Gilad Feldman
  • 4be3331 [main] sync from escicheckapp@f52d7ce (effectcheck v0.6.3) (#17) — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →
